summary.what: Made decision-log ADR numbering durable via {{vars.starting_number}} with caller-owned offset
summary.why: ADRs are meant to be stable identifiers (ADR-12 should always mean the same decision); hardcoded restart-at-1 broke that and caused external references to rot silently
summary.how: Added 'vars:' map to template frontmatter for per-template defaults; decision-log sets starting_number default to 1. Render applies caller vars first, then template defaults fill remaining {{vars.*}} tokens. Tutorial documents the append-with-offset workflow; 'just decision-log' recipe greps max ADR-N from target file and passes next via --var.

notes: Chose per-template defaults in frontmatter over hardcoded defaults in render.go to avoid coupling the render package to specific template variables. Only considered briefly: baking a 'continue-from <file>' flag into draft itself, but that embeds decision-log-specific parsing logic in a generic command — the justfile recipe is the right place for that sugar.
